Class AlcoholLogService

java.lang.Object
ntnu.no.fs_v26.service.AlcoholLogService
@Service public class AlcoholLogService extends Object
Service class for alcohol compliance log operations.

Handles creation and retrieval of alcohol log entries as part of the IK-Alkohol module. Enforces Norwegian alcohol serving regulations, including:

  • Age requirements (18+ for beverages under 22%, 20+ for beverages at 22% or above)
  • Serving time windows (low ABV from 08:00–01:00, high ABV from 13:00–23:59)
  • Serving state validation (age checks and incidents require an active serving period)

Deviations are automatically created when compliance violations are detected.

  • Constructor Details

    • AlcoholLogService

      public AlcoholLogService(AlcoholLogRepository alcoholLogRepository, UserRepository userRepository, DeviationRepository deviationRepository)

  • Method Details

    • createLog

      public AlcoholLogResponse createLog(AlcoholLogRequest request, String userEmail)
      Creates and saves a new alcohol log entry for the current shift.

      Validates the serving state and request fields before saving. Automatically creates a deviation if a compliance violation is detected.

      Parameters:
      request - the log request body
      userEmail - the email of the authenticated user creating the log
      Returns:
      the saved log as an AlcoholLogResponse
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if validation fails (e.g. missing fields, serving not started)

    • getCurrentDayLogs

      public List<AlcoholLogResponse> getCurrentDayLogs(String userEmail)
      Returns all alcohol log entries recorded for the current day in the user's organization.
      Parameters:
      userEmail - the email of the authenticated user
      Returns:
      a list of today's log entries as AlcoholLogResponse objects

    • getLogsByDate

      public List<AlcoholLogResponse> getLogsByDate(String userEmail, LocalDate date)
      Returns alcohol log entries for a specific date in the user's organization. Only accessible to users with the MANAGER or ADMIN role.
      Parameters:
      userEmail - the email of the authenticated user
      date - the date to retrieve logs for
      Returns:
      a list of log entries for the given date
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the user does not have the required role

    • hasLogsForToday

      public boolean hasLogsForToday(String userEmail)
      Checks whether any alcohol logs have been recorded today in the user's organization. Used by the dashboard to indicate compliance status.
      Parameters:
      userEmail - the email of the authenticated user
      Returns:
      true if at least one log exists for today
